items.title: Liquidity structure | Let's put speed profile into context
items.subtitle: Basic concepts, Liquidity, TPO chart, Speed profile
items.description: Basic concepts, Liquidity, TPO chart, Speed profile
items.truncated_body_text: During the 2021 GameStop gamma squeeze, the retail crowd discovered the options market and the hedging activity of market makers. The newly discovered “low – the idea that options positioning can directly impact the price of the underlying asset – went through multiple, almost conspiratorial misinterpretations. On the back of this, a lot of furus jumped into building gamma/delta etc. data-providing services, operating on the old “sell shovels during a gold rush” principle.

items.title: The "Bessent put" - SPX/yields correlation; (Hidden QE)
items.description: After calling the April selloff and the downside target in advance, I began writing more and more about a so-called ‘hidden QE’—which, by May, I started referring to as the ‘Bessent put’: a concealed support mechanism designed to sustain artificial growth, primarily via equity–yield correlation dynamics.
items.truncated_body_text: After calling the April selloff and the downside target in advance, I began writing more and more about a so-called ‘hidden QE’—which, by May, I started referring to as the ‘Bessent put’: a concealed support mechanism designed to sustain artificial growth, primarily via equity–yield correlation dynamics. As someone who both trades tactically in the short term and manages a long-term investment portfolio, this metric is one of my most important gauges of market sentiment.

items.title: A Guide to Reading My Daily Posts
items.social_title: A Guide to Reading My Daily Posts
items.description: Every time you look at tools such as put/call deltas, ratios, or even monitor changes in order flow (e.g., the ratio between selling and buying volume), you get lagging data.

items.title: Statistical-arbitrage: Mean Reversion Trading
items.subtitle: Course: Cointegration, ADF, CADF, Johansen-test, Risk management
items.description: Course: Cointegration, ADF, CADF, Johansen-test, Risk management
items.truncated_body_text: In the following educational post, I will examine mean-reversion trading. I will introduce cointegration, the Augmented Dickey–Fuller test, the Johansen test, the mathematics of half-life, and my own method for incorporating the realised distribution (skewness and kurtosis).

items.title: My trading principles Pt. 2 - The Wall Street Casino and My Story
items.social_title: My trading principles Pt. 2 - The Wall Street Casino and My Story
items.subtitle: Turning negative edge into positive edge
items.description: Turning negative edge into positive edge
items.truncated_body_text: The market’s system is structured so that most trading strategies are sure to be loss-making in the long run. This is similar to a casino, except that the negative edge comes together in a much more complex way.

items.title: My basic trading principles Pt. 1 - Strategies, basic risk management
items.description: In this post I will examine the psychological aspects of trading.
items.truncated_body_text: In this post I will examine the psychological aspects of trading. I’ll briefly illustrate—through my own experience—the essence of technical analysis and position analysis, explain how working people can easily determine the amount they can allocate to trading, and share my core practical principles for directional trading

items.title: Odds, Fair Bet and Kelly Criterion (position sizing)
items.subtitle: Moderate but necessary-math warning!
items.truncated_body_text: In my previous posts, you encountered the concepts of "fair play" or "fair bet." In this post, I’m going to introduce this concept in more detail, as it is one of the fundamental principles of option pricing and a key concept in risk management. I will also introduce a bet sizing mathematical model known as the Kelly criterion.

items.title: What is volatility?
items.subtitle: Math warning!
items.truncated_body_text: In this article, I'm going to introduce the concept of volatility, log return, and standard deviation, providing the mathematical background to help you gain a better understanding of these concepts. I will also introduce the four main volatility formulas to illustrate the evolution of volatility estimation: Parkinson, Garman-Klass, Roger-Satchell, and Yang-Zhang.
